title: notelaea flavicans
intro: Notelaea flavicans, also known as the yellow bell, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the coastal regions of eastern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Notelaea flavicans include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Notelaea flavicans can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
